# Sub PEQ filters
# miniDSP-style peaking filters plus normalized biquad coefficients
# Biquad convention: y[n] = b0*x[n] + b1*x[n-1] + b2*x[n-2] - a1*y[n-1] - a2*y[n-2]

Filter 1: ON PK Fc 57.674 Hz Gain -8.886 dB Q 7.5111
  biquad b0=0.99973169354 b1=-1.99914793748 b2=0.999430486926 a1=-1.99914793748 a2=0.999162180466
Filter 2: ON PK Fc 146.300 Hz Gain -7.992 dB Q 5.5723
  biquad b0=0.999182365338 b1=-1.99718994896 b2=0.998099145291 a1=-1.99718994896 a2=0.99728151063
Filter 3: ON PK Fc 100.918 Hz Gain +3.000 dB Q 3.0710
  biquad b0=1.00037293721 b1=-1.99814839694 b2=0.997819046917 a1=-1.99814839694 a2=0.998191984131
Filter 4: ON PK Fc 80.834 Hz Gain -5.013 dB Q 7.9832
  biquad b0=0.999806168259 b1=-1.99908799791 b2=0.99930980731 a1=-1.99908799791 a2=0.999115975569
Filter 5: ON PK Fc 48.043 Hz Gain +0.655 dB Q 5.8135
  biquad b0=1.0000204051 b1=-1.99946939946 b2=0.999458878997 a1=-1.99946939946 a2=0.999479284097
